summary: PR #2529 implemented and/or improved many gm_1601.c stub functions, updated gm_1601.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to recovered signatures, and added small struct field recoveries in gm/types.h. The bot report recorded 2 newly matched functions, 18 unmatched-item improvements, and one small unmatched regression.

key_files.role: Main decompilation body: replaces many stub markers with C implementations, adds includes, noinline-style wrapper helpers, match/stat/name-tag logic, and some typed field accesses.
Prototype updates for recovered gm_1601 functions, replacing several unknown signatures.
Type recovery for fields used by gm_1601 matching work.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t gains x8, x9, x1C fields; MatchPlayerData gains x38 and x3C fields

what_changed: Implemented or improved gm_1601.c functions named in the PR body, including fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, gm_8016AC44, fn_80169A84, fn_80169C54, fn_80169000, fn_80167638, fn_80161C90, fn_80161154, fn_8016A4C8, gm_80166CCC, and fn_80164B48.
Added includes for placeholder.h, if/ifnametag.h, and pl/plbonus.h to support the new gm_1601.c code.
Removed a local #pragma dont_inline block around gm_801603B0 and introduced wrapper helpers such as gm_801601C4_noinline and gm_80160244_noinline.
Collapsed gm_80160C90 language/name selection into calls through gm_80160980_noinline3 and fn_801609E0_noinline3.
Added fn_80160DE8 text/name rendering logic using lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, and HSD_SisLib_803A7548.
Added a local struct gm_stats used by fn_80161C90 for saturated accumulation of player match statistics.
Updated gm_80162574 from raw byte-offset pointer arithmetic on gmMainLib_8015EDBC() to field access through x18.
Updated gm_1601.h prototypes for recovered return types and parameters, including replacements for UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Added fields in gm/types.h for lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t and MatchPlayerData that the new implementations access.

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were updated alongside body recovery instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS behind.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: corresponding prototype updates in gm_1601.h

smart_moves.text: At least one raw offset expression was converted into a named struct field access, improving reviewability.
The PR split focused gm_1601 work out of a larger branch, which made the slice reviewable as gm_1601-specific progress.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a signature, update the owning header in the same PR so call sites and prototypes do not retain UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.h converts several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete s32, u32, void, bool, and typed-parameter signatures

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large stats/update functions, introducing narrow recovered fields or a temporary local struct can make repeated offset updates readable, but unknown field names should remain conservative until semantics are proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local struct gm_stats with unk-offset fields in fn_80161C90
raw_diff: types.h adds x8, x9, x1C, x38, x3C fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use project macros for saturating arithmetic when they express the recovered operation; this PR repeatedly uses MIN for capped counters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80161C90, fn_80162170, gm_8016260C use MIN or explicit 0xFFFF caps

naming_conventions.pattern: Address-style function names were preserved for functions without proven semantic names.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: function list keeps address-style names
raw_diff: implemented functions retain address-style names

naming_conventions.pattern: Recovered but not semantically understood struct fields use offset-like xNN or unkNN names rather than guessed names.
Matching helper wrappers in this PR use suffixes such as _inner, _noinline, _noinline2, and _noinline3.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: wrapper helper additions in gm_1601.c

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: No-inline steering was attempted by wrapper call chains after removing a scoped #pragma dont_inline block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removes #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop around gm_801603B0
raw_diff: adds gm_801601C4_inner/noinline and gm_80160244_inner/noinline wrappers
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The loaded evidence shows the tactic existed, but does not prove it should be a general standard. Current standards require narrow verifier evidence for such steering.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Ternary selection replaced a nested language/name branch ladder in gm_80160C90.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_80160C90 nested if block replaced by ternary calls to fn_801609E0_noinline3 or gm_80160980_noinline3

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Field recovery was used to replace at least one raw pointer offset while preserving indexed access shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_80162574 x18 indexed field access

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Nested natural loops were used for player/team ranking and persistent name-tag stat updates.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80162170 loops over players and opponents

notes: The raw diff loaded in the prompt was truncated, but targeted local reads provided the header and types.h changes.
There are no human review comments in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale are not inferred.
The bot report gives matching/progress evidence, but not detailed objdiff rationale for each tactic.
Several implemented functions remained partial improvements rather than complete matches according to the bot report; only gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8 are reported as new 100% matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR #2529 gm_1601 matching progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2529 implemented/matched gm_1601.c stubs with prototype and type updates; automated report showed 2 new matches, 18 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 small unmatched regression.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prototype cleanup accompanied gm_1601 body recovery
accepted_candidate_records.text: In this PR, recovered gm_1601 function bodies were paired with gm_1601.h signature updates, replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS entries with typed prototypes.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1601.c contains match-end/stat/name-tag game-mode logic
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2529 evidence shows src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c work around MatchEnd, MatchPlayerData, ranking/winner selection, persistent NameTagData stat updates, language-specific fighter-name text, and game-mode match counters.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scope fact for future gm_1601 searches; should be curated against current source before acceptance.

source_update_candidates.title: Document noinline wrapper chains as a cautionary matching tactic if more PRs support it
source_update_candidates.text: This PR used wrapper chains named _inner/_noinline/_noinline2/_noinline3 and removed a local #pragma dont_inline block. The evidence supports recording the tactic as present in this PR, but not as a general accepted standard without additional verifier and review evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801603B0 pragma removal and noinline wrapper calls
raw_diff: gm_80160980_noinline3 and fn_801609E0_noinline3 wrapper chains
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential standards note or caution, but current evidence is too narrow to promote automatically.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the noinline wrapper chain pattern as an accepted global style from this PR alone; the loaded evidence lacks function-by-function objdiff proof and human review rationale.
Do not infer semantic names for xNN or unkNN fields; the PR preserves conservative names and the evidence does not prove full field meanings.
Do not claim all PR-body functions fully matched; the bot report only lists gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8 as new 100% matches, with many others as partial improvements.
